fact: February 3 is exactly 34 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 9% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are starting to wake up from their winter naps around this time, getting ready to check for their shadows the day before!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier than it did just a week ago in most places - that's like getting an extra 14 minutes of morning light in just one week!
If you're born on February 3, you're an Aquarius, which means your zodiac symbol is a water bearer even though Aquarius is actually an air sign - talk about confusing!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, February 3 falls right in the middle of their hottest summer days when cicadas are buzzing so loudly they can drown out conversations!
Tree sap in cold places is starting to move again around this date, which is why maple syrup makers get excited - the trees are literally waking up from winter!
February 3 is exactly 46 days before the spring equinox, making it the perfect middle point between deep winter and the first day of spring!
Male cardinals start singing their loudest, most show-offy songs around this time to attract mates, turning quiet winter mornings into birdy concerts!
When there's a full moon on February 3, it's called the 'Snow Moon' because February typically brings the heaviest snowfalls in northern climates!
In many places, February 3 marks when daylight lasts about 30 minutes longer than it did at the winter solstice - like getting a free half-hour TV show of sunlight!
Earthworms in warmer climates start moving closer to the surface around this date, making early February the secret best time for fishing in some places!
February 3 falls during the coldest statistical period in the Northern Hemisphere, when even places that don't get much snow might see their breath in the morning air!
